scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Meets most pass criteria (9 rules extracted, 5+ clarifying questions via escalations, 'demand' explicitly flagged as undefined, confidence at 35, no specific values invented) but critically fails the 'No Given/When/Then criteria written' requirement — AC-1 through AC-6 are full Given/When/Then acceptance criteria written against vague input, which is an explicit failure mode in the rubric. The criteria reference 'configured' values rather than inventing specifics, but their mere presence contradicts the expected behavior of refusing to write acceptance criteria when inputs are too vague.

observations.text: Extracted 9 business rules (BR-1 through BR-9), well exceeding the 5-rule minimum, with invariant classifications and justifications
Confidence level at 35 meets the <= 40 threshold and reasoning is thorough, correctly characterizing the input as 'a vision statement, not a spec'
5 detailed escalations surface the critical clarifying questions: demand definition, guardrail values, pricing function shape, real-time SLA, and scope of applicability
'Demand' is explicitly identified as undefined in escalations, low_confidence_areas, and confidence reasoning
No specific numeric values invented — rules and criteria reference 'configured' parameters rather than fabricating thresholds
Wrote 6 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria (AC-1 through AC-6) despite the rubric requiring none be written — this is listed as both a failure mode and a pass-blocking criterion
Produced a comprehensive domain analysis (full business_impact section with financial/operational/user/risk analysis and 6 stakeholder groups) that treats the input as more specified than it is
Business rules lack individual confidence ratings — they have invariant flags but no per-rule confidence level as expected by the rubric
8 detailed edge cases suggest deeper analysis than warranted for a vague soundbite input — the volume of analysis implies more certainty than exists
